subject: Sociology
title: The Future of Sociology
description: A 1-minute de-risking session on The Future of Sociology.

hook.text: Algorithms decide who gets loans, jobs, and parole. The newest sociological frontier is code, not just culture.

buildup.text: Digital sociology studies how technology reshapes inequality, identity, and community in the 21st century.

discovery.text: Big data gives sociologists tools their founders never imagined — but also raises new ethical dilemmas.

twist.text: Algorithms reproduce bias. If training data reflects racism, the algorithm automates it at scale.

climax.text: Sociology's core mission hasn't changed: understand how social forces shape human life. The forces just went digital.

punchline.text: The questions are the same. The battlefield moved online.

quiz.question: What is a key concern of digital sociology?
options: That algorithms can reproduce and scale existing biases
That the internet will replace all social interaction
That sociology is no longer relevant
